title: TOON toon-client pod Publish Contract
description: Wire-shape contract between the persistent Akash toon-client pod (story 49.3) and its consumers (49.4 settlement smoke, 49.5 live e2e gate, third-party developers). Drift = build break. See _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/49-3-persistent-akash-foreign-client-pod.md § 'Schema-Contract Discipline'.
$comment: Idempotency is handled at the Nostr layer (event.id = SHA-256 of canonical event). Pod is stateless w.r.t. replay — retries MUST reuse the same signed event object (re-stamping created_at produces a new event.id which bypasses relay dedup).

Hex64.description: 64-char lowercase hex — 32-byte SHA-256 hash, secp256k1 pubkey, or Schnorr signature half.

AnyoneHostname.description: v3 base32 onion-equivalent hostname (admits both .anyone canonical apex and .anon locally-published HS TLDs; alphabet [a-z2-7] preserves the rate-limit-via-shape guard). MUST be sourced from the operator's ~/.townhouse/host.json or a published kind:10032 event.

id.description: SHA-256 of the canonical event serialization. The pod does NOT recompute or verify this; the caller is trusted to produce a well-formed event.

pubkey.description: 32-byte x-only Schnorr pubkey (BIP-340) — the caller's Nostr identity, NOT the pod's ephemeral signer key.

created_at.description: Unix seconds. Relays may reject events outside their drift window.

sig.description: BIP-340 Schnorr signature over the canonical event.

eventId.description: Echoed event.id from the request. The relay deduplicates by this value; a successful 202 confirms the relay accepted (or already had) the event.

claimHash.description: First 32 bytes of the EIP-712 balance-proof signature, 0x-prefixed. Provided for caller-side correlation with on-chain settlement; opaque otherwise.

chainId.description: Settlement chain id (e.g., 31337 for Anvil-style devnet).

publishedAt.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the pod finished the publish (after relay acceptance receipt).

durationMs.description: Wall-clock duration from request receipt to relay acceptance, in milliseconds.

error.description: Human-readable error message. Stable enough to log; treat as opaque for branching.

field.description: Body field that triggered the error (e.g., 'targetHostname'). Only set for shape-validation failures.

ajvErrors.description: Ajv error array — only present when the request body failed schema validation. Surfaces the exact path+keyword that mismatched so the caller can correct.

retryAfterSec.description: Seconds the caller should wait before retrying. Matches the Retry-After header.

error.description: Human-readable error message — typically the relay's NACK reason or a transport-level failure.

relayAck.description: Verbatim relay acknowledgement string if the relay responded but rejected the event. Absent on transport failures.

retryable.description: True for transient errors (timeout, transport reset). False for permanent rejects (event malformed, balance insufficient). Caller MAY retry the SAME event object on retryable=true; re-signing would change event.id and bypass dedup.

anyoneReady.description: True once the in-pod anon SOCKS5 daemon has bound its listener AND the pod has been funded by the 49.2 faucet.

evmAddr.description: Ephemeral EVM address generated in memory at pod boot. NOT persisted; lost on restart.

solAddr.description: Ephemeral Solana pubkey (base58) generated in memory at pod boot. NOT persisted; lost on restart.

evm.description: EVM balance in wei as a decimal string (avoids JSON precision loss).

sol.description: Solana lamport balance. Fits safely in a JS number for devnet-scale amounts.

bootedAt.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp captured AFTER faucet auto-fund completed (NOT process-start time).

socksProxy.description: MUST start with socks5h:// (NOT socks5://) to prevent DNS leaks of .anyone hostnames. Mirrors packages/client/src/config.ts:111-115.
